extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked with 2.5km remaining on the 7km final climb at La Loge des Gardes, bridged to GAUDU's earlier move, dropped him in final kilometer to win and take yellow.
tactical_patterns: UAE Emirates domestiques chased breakaway all day signaling Pogačar's aggressive intentions before uphill finish rather than conserving energy.
Intermediate sprint bonus seconds contested 7km before summit finish showing GC teams willing to expend matches twice in finale.
Gaudu attacked at 3.5km to go attempting to preempt Pogačar/Vingegaard marked duel, forcing Pogačar to bridge alone.
INEOS led into climb base but immediately ceded to Gall's Austrian champion Großschartner showing weak mountain train depth.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: attacked first at 5km but couldn't follow Pogačar's bridge to Gaudu, finished 44 seconds down revealing March form deficit
rider_signals.GAUDU David: positive: solo attack 3.5km out forced Pogačar to work for stage win, held second at 10 seconds demonstrating Tour leadership credentials
rider_signals.GALL Felix: positive: Großschartner as Austrian champion set tempo on climb for Gall's GC suggesting AG2R depth beyond Gaudu
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: negative: yellow jersey unable to hold position on 7km at 7.1% exposing weight/climbing limitations for post-Classics shape
rider_signals.VOKRRI Kevin: positive: finished ahead of Vingegaard at 44 seconds as young rider showing emerging GC potential in mountain finishes
